metadata.source_paper: Lestari, H., Supendi, P., Nugraha, A. D., & Heryandoko, N. (2021). Preliminary results: tomographic imaging of P-wave velocity structure beneath Kalimantan, Makassar Strait, and Sulawesi using Fast Marching Tomography Method. ITB Graduate School Conference (IGSC), December 21, 2021.
metadata.comprehension_summary: Preliminary P-wave tomography using 85 BMKG seismic stations (Jan 2017-Jan 2021) covering Kalimantan, Makassar Strait, and Sulawesi with FMTOMO software (fast-marching method). Confirms Sulawesi has significantly higher seismicity than Kalimantan due to active faults (PKF, Matano) and NST subduction. High-velocity anomalies expected to image subducted slabs (NST, Sangihe, Halmahera). Preliminary study — full tomographic images not described in text (conference proceedings, 8 pages).
main_conclusions: Sulawesi has significantly higher seismicity than Kalimantan due to PKF, Matano Fault, and NST subduction zone
FMTOMO (fast-marching tomography) applied to P-wave travel times
High-velocity anomalies expected to correspond to subducted slabs (NST Celebes Sea slab, Sangihe, Halmahera)
Preliminary results — full interpretation pending further analysis
figures_used: Fig. 1 (study area + earthquake epicenters + BMKG stations)

properties.name: Área de estudio — Tomografía P-wave Kalimantan-Makassar-Sulawesi
properties.full_name: Study area — P-wave tomography Kalimantan + Makassar Strait + Sulawesi, 85 BMKG stations, Jan 2017-Jan 2021
properties.resolution_note: Preliminary results — resolution not formally assessed in this proceedings paper
properties.source_apa: Lestari et al. (2021). ITB Graduate School Conference (IGSC).
properties.paper_finding: P-wave tomography applied to Kalimantan-Makassar-Sulawesi region; high seismicity of Sulawesi vs Kalimantan documented; slabs (NST, Sangihe, Halmahera) expected as high-V anomalies
properties.extraction_notes: Extent estimado del área de estudio basado en descripción del paper (cubre Kalimantan, Estrecho de Makassar, Sulawesi). Polígono amplio incluyendo toda la región.

conclusions.text: P-wave tomography confirms high seismicity of Sulawesi vs Kalimantan; subducted slabs (NST Celebes Sea, Sangihe, Halmahera) expected to appear as high-velocity anomalies
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La tomografía P regional Kalimantan-Sulawesi puede revelar la geometría de las losas en el estrecho de Makassar — potencial identificación de losa relicta del rift Makassar bajo la cuenca

properties.name: Estaciones BMKG — 85 estaciones sismológicas (representativas)
properties.full_name: BMKG Seismic Stations (85 stations) used in P-wave tomography Kalimantan-Makassar-Sulawesi
properties.source_apa: Lestari et al. (2021). ITB Graduate School Conference (IGSC).
properties.paper_finding: 85 BMKG stations used for P-wave travel time inversion covering Kalimantan, Makassar Strait, and Sulawesi
properties.extraction_notes: NOTA: Coordenadas exactas de las 85 estaciones no listadas en el texto. Estos 10 puntos son representativos de ubicaciones conocidas de redes BMKG. Para las 85 estaciones reales: descargar de IRIS FDSN Station (red IA, ver api-integration.md tarea s2_api2).

conclusions.text: 85 BMKG stations provide P-wave coverage for Kalimantan-Makassar-Sulawesi; Sulawesi's denser seismicity provides better ray coverage than Kalimantan
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La asimetría de cobertura sísmica (alta en Sulawesi, baja en Kalimantan) limita la resolución tomográfica bajo Kalimantan — las anomalías bajo el estrecho de Makassar serán las menos resueltas
